design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character with unique movement profile → navigate dream world rooms via doors/ladders → pull vegetables and grab enemies to throw at other enemies → reach door to advance level → defeat boss → progress to next world
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing character-specific abilities (speed vs jump height vs strength vs float capacity) against environmental hazards and enemy placement; resource scarcity (health hearts, limited continues)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Explore a surreal dreamland with dynamic traversal options; defeat threats through environmental manipulation rather than direct jumping attacks
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radically breaks Mario formula by removing jumping-on-enemies as primary attack; mandates pre-level character selection that locks you into distinct mechanical trade-offs; includes no time limit and vertical scrolling, establishing it as experimental reinvention rather than sequel. Based on licensed Doki Doki Panic reskin, yet defined its own identity through mechanics-first design philosophy that prioritized character asymmetry and item-throwing combat over familiar jump-stomp patterns.

difficulty.lives_system: Start with 3 lives; lose one life when health (hearts) depleted or character falls off screen
difficulty.continues_system: Player may continue up to twice in one game after Game Over
difficulty.checkpointing: No explicit checkpoint system; progression through worlds is linear with warp doors in Sub-space allowing level skipping
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: character selection before each level directly modulates difficulty (Toad fastest/weakest jump, Luigi highest jump/slowest, Princess can float, Mario balanced). Sub-space warp system provides optional difficulty reduction by enabling level skipping. Health management via enemy defeat spawning floating hearts creates dynamic difficulty pacing.

progression.upgrade_system: Mushrooms collected in Sub-space temporarily increase max health; no permanent stat upgrades. Character selection acts as 'loadout' system with inherent mechanical trade-offs.
progression.unlock_mechanism: 7 worlds with 20 levels total (6 worlds with 3 levels each, World 7 with 2 levels). Boss doors at end of Worlds 1-6. Wart confrontation in World 7 as final gate. Certain jars in Sub-space unlock warp passages to later worlds.
replayability_hooks: Character asymmetry incentivizes replaying levels with different protagonists to discover optimal character-specific routes
Sub-space warp discovery and challenge routes (speedrunners use intentional damage and glitch abuse for optimization)
Bonus Chance minigame uses coins from Sub-space collection as currency, creating secondary progression loop
Multiple themed worlds (desert, snow, etc.) with distinct obstacle sets encourage exploration variance

world_building_techniques: Dream-logic framing: Mario dreams of staircase → voice identifies Subcon dreamland → requests aid against Wart
Thematic world design: each of 7 worlds has distinct visual/mechanical theme (desert with quicksand, snow with slippery surfaces) reinforcing dream geography
Environmental storytelling: jars, doors, Sub-space reflected areas, and magical potions establish magical dream logic without explicit exposition
Pre-game narrative hook: game opens with Mario's dream sequence establishing the emotional premise before gameplay begins

boss_design.philosophy: Each world culminates in a thematic boss encounter (Worlds 1-6) that requires learning enemy-specific attack patterns and character-appropriate counter-strategies. Bosses are vulnerable to thrown enemies or environmental objects rather than jumping; final boss Wart has documented invincibility-star vulnerability. Boss design philosophy emphasizes pattern recognition and resource management over twitch reflexes.
notable_bosses: Mouser (World 1): vulnerable to mushroom blocks and thrown enemies; can be defeated with invincibility star
Birdo (appears multiple times): throws eggs that can be thrown back; requires timing and positioning
Wart (World 7 final boss): primary antagonist; vulnerable to specific attack methods documented in speedrun optimization

faction_indicators: Character selection creates implicit playstyle tribes: Toad mains (speed-runners), Luigi mains (jump-high specialists), Princess mains (float-dependent), Mario mains (balanced traditionalists)
danger_signals: Dream/nightmare framing could enable dark mod communities
Subcon as foreign dreamland allows alternate-reality themed fan content
Wart as tyrant antagonist enables political allegory in community discussion
environmental_storytelling: Sub-space reflects player's main world progression, creating visual metaphor for conscious/unconscious mind duality
Jar entrances establish hidden-passage discovery as core world-interaction mechanic
Mushroom blocks and pulled vegetables as physics objects create environmental puzzle-solving narrative of player agency over dream logic
Warp doors in Sub-space serve both mechanical (skip levels) and narrative (dreamland shortcuts) functions
